preamble: The shift from the voiceless alveolar lateral fricative [ɬ] to its voiced counterpart [ɮ] is a form of voicing lenition. This typically occurs in intervocalic environments or through grammaticalized consonant mutations.

examples.note: Contextual voicing of the lateral fricative in certain positions.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
